summary: Refactored fighter draw/common rendering and collision-debug draw code to improve ftdrawcommon matching. The PR made ftDrawCommon_8008051C and ftDrawCommon_80080E18 fully match, improved ftDrawCommon_800805C8 and ftDrawCommon_80080C28 to 99.90%, and cleaned up related fighter and lb collision/dynamics types so call sites use typed arrays and bool parameters instead of placeholder pointers and decompiler-style pointer arithmetic.
what_changed: Changed ftDrawCommon_800805C8 signature from taking s32 arg2 to bool arg2 in ftdrawcommon.c and ftdrawcommon.h, and updated callers in ftDrawCommon_80080E18 to pass true/false explicitly.

Replaced much of ftDrawCommon_800805C8's decompiler-style temporaries and pointer stepping with typed fighter access via GET_FIGHTER, for loops over x914, hurt_capsules, x1670, dynamic_bone_sets, and x1614, and a named do_invalidate flag before HSD_StateInvalidate(-1).
Factored repeated view-matrix setup into ftDrawCommon_8008051C and reused it from ftDrawCommon_800805C8, ftDrawCommon_80080C28, and new ftDrawCommon_80080E18 helper inlines.
Introduced static const GXColor values ftDrawCommon_804D836C and ftDrawCommon_804D8370 instead of extern s32 arrays, and passed them as GXColor pointers to lb_800117F4.

Split ftDrawCommon_80080E18's repeated draw paths into static inline helpers ftDrawCommon_80080E18_inline0, ftDrawCommon_80080E18_inline1, and ftDrawCommon_80080E18_inline2 while preserving camera-mode branching.

Refined Fighter layout around offsets 0x1614 and 0x1670: x1614 became an array of two 0x2C-byte structs; x166C was documented as the number of valid x1670 entries; x1670 became a Fighter_x1670_t array with Vec3, float, HSD_JObj pointer, and padding.
Added a forward declaration for Fighter_x1670_t in ft/forward.h and moved lbColl_8000A460's parameter from a local lbcollision Foo placeholder to Fighter_x1670_t*.
Changed lb_800117F4's third argument type from UNK_T to GXColor* in lb_00F9.c/h and simplified a goto-based branch into an else-if guard.
smart_moves: Using ARRAY_SIZE for fixed fighter arrays such as x914 and x1614 made loop bounds explicit and avoided fragile pointer-increment patterns.
Introducing Fighter_x1670_t at the fighter layout site let lbcollision.h drop the local Foo placeholder while keeping lbColl_8000A460 accurately typed against fighter data.
Reusing ftDrawCommon_8008051C for view-matrix construction reduced duplicated stack-sensitive matrix code in multiple draw routines and helped ftDrawCommon_8008051C reach 100%.
Changing local color storage in ftDrawCommon_800805C8 from float-like temporaries to GXColor variables aligned the caller with lb_800117F4's actual color-pointer usage.
Splitting ftDrawCommon_80080E18 into static inline helpers preserved inlining-sensitive code shape while making the camera-case rendering paths easier to reason about.
Replacing magic integer truth values with bool parameters and true/false call sites clarified that ftDrawCommon_800805C8's third parameter controls draw behavior, not a numeric render mode.
decomp_lessons: When an unmatched function repeatedly constructs the same view matrix, factor the construction into a shared helper only if the resulting call/inlining behavior matches; here ftDrawCommon_8008051C became the reusable matching primitive.
Struct layout refinements can unlock cleaner matching: defining x1614 and x1670 arrays in Fighter removed misleading arithmetic such as fighter + 0x28 or fighter + 0x4C style stepping.
Do not leave placeholder types in unrelated library headers when the data is owned by a concrete system; forward-declare the owning type and point APIs at that type.
For match-sensitive rendering code, static inline helper extraction can reproduce repeated source patterns without necessarily adding call overhead.
Replacing gotos with structured conditionals is viable when it preserves branch semantics; lb_800117F4 kept the same arg4 behavior while removing block_5.
Small type corrections, such as UNK_T to GXColor* and s32 to bool, can improve both readability and match quality across caller/callee boundaries.
naming_conventions: New fighter substructure followed the existing offset-derived placeholder style: Fighter_x1670_t for the struct stored at Fighter offset 0x1670.
Unknown fighter fields retained x-offset names such as x166C, x1670, x21FC_flag, x2223_b2, and x2227_b7, with comments documenting known meaning where available.
Temporary matching helper names remained local and descriptive but provisional, e.g. mtx_thing_2 and ftDrawCommon_80080E18_inline0/inline1/inline2.
Boolean locals and parameters were named for behavior where known, e.g. do_invalidate and bool arg2, even when the public function name stayed address-based.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a #pragma dont_inline wrapper around ftDrawCommon_8008051C after rewriting the matrix helper and locals into a naturally matching form.
Used explicit PAD_STACK calls and carefully placed local matrices/GXColor variables to maintain stack layout in match-sensitive draw functions.

Replaced pointer-induction variables from decompiler output with indexed for loops over typed arrays while preserving bounds casts such as (unsigned) fighter->hurt_capsules_len and (unsigned) fighter->x166C.
Centralized PSMTXIdentity, translation-column writes, HSD_CObjGetCurrent()->view_mtx access, and PSMTXConcat in ftDrawCommon_8008051C.
Changed ftDrawCommon_800805C8 to early-return when x21FC_flag.b7 is false, simplifying nested control flow while keeping the draw-path behavior.
Used static const GXColor objects for rodata/data matching instead of extern s32 arrays for ftDrawCommon_804D836C and ftDrawCommon_804D8370.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

decomp.dev bot reported matched code improved by +940 bytes overall: ftDrawCommon_8008051C reached 100% from 67.19%, ftDrawCommon_80080E18 reached 100% from 99.96%, ftDrawCommon_800805C8 improved from 89.76% to 99.90%, and ftDrawCommon_80080C28 improved from 99.03% to 99.90%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve the @todo for the proper size or real name of Fighter_x1670_t.
Search for subsequent work bringing ftDrawCommon_800805C8 and ftDrawCommon_80080C28 from 99.90% to 100%.
Audit other lbcollision APIs for placeholder structs similar to the removed Foo type.
Check whether ftDrawCommon_80080E18_inline0/1/2 later received semantic names or were folded into named draw-mode helpers.
Search for other callers of lb_800117F4 to confirm the GXColor* third argument remained stable.
